mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over rolling 3-year windows, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and residential investment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of monetary easing reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and output growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during easing cycles do not yield positive 5-day forward returns, with a t-statistic below 1.5 for the mean return difference.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% monthly are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm broad-based labor market recovery, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a cumulative information coefficient below 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and higher corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 20-day forward returns, with out-of-sample R-squared consistently below 0.005.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production exceeding 1.0% signals immediate supply-side stress and inventory drawdowns, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO drops greater than 1.0% are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in over 55% of observed instances.
